meta.description: Complete scoring logic for all question types. Includes rules, edge cases, and Python pseudocode for each scoring mode.

scoring_rules.label: Binary Scoring
rules: Correct answer selected → score = 1
Incorrect answer selected → score = 0
No answer → score = 0
edge_cases: For audio/video/flash/passage/image multi-sub-question types (1c–1h): each sub-question is scored independently as BINARY
For hotspot single (5a): only the LATEST click is evaluated; earlier clicks are discarded

scoring_rules.label: Binary Scoring with Fuzzy/Percentage Match
rules: Mode A — Exact match against list: if candidate text matches any of up to 5 predefined correct answers → score = 1
Mode B — Percentage match: single correct answer; candidate text matched letter-by-letter; if match% >= threshold → score = 1
Case sensitivity: optional; configurable at question creation
Unless capitalisation is explicitly required, upper/lowercase treated as equivalent
edge_cases: Mode A and Mode B are mutually exclusive; selection made at question creation
In Mode B at 100% threshold: every character must match exactly (modulo case setting)
pseudocode: # Mode A: match any in list
return 1 if candidate.strip() in [c.strip() for c in correct_list] else 0
# Mode B: percentage match against single correct answer
matches = sum(a == b for a, b in zip(candidate, correct))

scoring_rules.label: Partial Credit Scoring
rules: Each scoreable item (field, match, recalled word/image) scored independently
Correct item → +1
Incorrect item → 0 (no negative)
Total score = sum of correct items
Maximum score = total number of items to complete
edge_cases: For FITB multi-field (2b): each field checked against its own correct answers list (up to 5 per field)
For recall types (2c, 2d): order of recall does not matter; any field matching any flashed item counts
For match-following (3): each pair checked independently
pseudocode: if candidate_value.strip().lower() in [c.strip().lower() for c in correct_list]:

scoring_rules.label: Negative Marking
rules: Each correct selection → +1
Each incorrect selection → -1
Final score = correct_count - incorrect_count
Floor rule: if incorrect_count > correct_count → score = 0 (never negative)
Not selecting an item (omission) → no penalty
edge_cases: For grid (4): only selected cells count; unselected cells have no penalty
For hotspot multi (5b): only CONFIRMED clicks count; cancelled clicks have no penalty
The floor prevents candidates being penalised below zero
pseudocode: correct_count = sum(1 for item in selected_items if item in correct_items_set)
incorrect_count = sum(1 for item in selected_items if item not in correct_items_set)

scoring_rules.label: Rank Scoring
rules: n options in the question; candidate assigns unique ranks 1 through n
Option assigned Rank 1 (highest) → score = n
Option assigned Rank 2 → score = n-1
Option assigned Rank n (lowest) → score = 1
Each option's score is stored against its section tag
edge_cases: Each rank must be unique per question (Rank 1 can only be assigned to one option)
All options must be ranked before submission

scoring_rules.label: Rank-then-Rate Scoring
rules: Two-level scoring per option:
Level 1: Rank score = (n - rank + 1)  e.g. Rank 1 of 4 → rank_score = 4
Level 2: Rating score = selected point on rating scale (e.g. 1–7)
Final score per option = rank_score × rating_score
Maximum per option = n × max_scale_point  (e.g. 4 × 7 = 28)
Each option's final score stored against its section tag
edge_cases: Both rank AND rating must be assigned for an option to be scored
Partial completion (only rank or only rating) should either block submission or score as 0
pseudocode: # Example: 4 options, option_B ranked 1 and rated 6

scoring_rules.label: Rating Scale Scoring
rules: Each question on the rating grid is scored by the scale point selected
Forward scoring: leftmost point = highest score (= n), rightmost = 1
Reverse scoring: rightmost point = highest score (= n), leftmost = 1
Middle points take progressively decreasing values
Unanswered items → score = 0 or excluded from total (configurable)
edge_cases: Forward vs reverse direction is set per question or per section at creation
Scale labels are descriptive (e.g. 'Very True' to 'Not at all True') but scores are numeric

scoring_rules.label: Forced-Choice Scoring
rules: Each question has exactly 2 options
Score values for each option are pre-defined at question creation
Score pairs may be: (0,1), (1,2), (1,3), or any custom pair
Candidate selects one option → that option's pre-defined score is assigned
Unselected option scores 0
Scores are accumulated per section (via option → section tag)
edge_cases: Unlike rating, scores are not always 0 and 1
If candidate does not answer, both options score 0

scoring_rules.label: Forced-Choice Two-Level Scoring
rules: Candidate selects one of two options (Level 1)
Candidate rates the selected option on a scale (Level 2)
Final score for selected option = predefined_option_score × rating
Unselected option scores 0
Scores accumulated per section

section_score_aggregation.description: How individual question scores roll up to section and assessment scores
rules: Each option/answer is tagged to a section at question creation
Section score = sum of all option scores tagged to that section
Assessment total = sum of all section scores
For psychometric types (6a, 6b, 7, 8a, 8b): section scores feed into profile computation (RVS, VMI, PMI, FMI etc.)
For cognitive types (1a–5b): section scores feed into aptitude/ability profiles

global_scoring_constraints: No score can go below 0 for any question (floor at 0 always applies)
Suspended and resumed sessions preserve all answered scores
